name: HappyFox Helpdesk REST API
what_it_does: Cloud helpdesk and IT service management platform with REST API for creating, updating, and managing support tickets — offering omnichannel support (email, chat, phone), automation workflows, and SLA management with comprehensive API access.
use_cases: Creating support tickets from monitoring alerts or CI/CD pipelines via REST API
Querying ticket status and SLA metrics for reporting and dashboard agents
Automating ticket routing and assignment based on external system events
Syncing customer data between HappyFox and CRM systems programmatically
Building escalation workflows that trigger HappyFox actions via API
not_for: Enterprise ITSM with complex change management (use ServiceNow)
Free or very low-cost helpdesk — HappyFox targets mid-market
Pure messaging-first platforms without structured ticket workflow needs
best_when: A mid-market team needs structured ticketing with SLA management, automation workflows, and REST API access for integration with internal systems.
avoid_when: You need open-source self-hosting, free tier for long-term use, or enterprise-grade ITSM complexity.

auth.notes: API key and secret from HappyFox account. HTTP Basic auth using key as username and secret as password. Account-scoped access. No granular permission scopes.

pricing.paid_tiers: Mighty from $26/agent/month; Fantastic from $36; Enterprise Pro from $52
pricing.notes: Per-agent pricing at mid-market point. No free tier — small teams should consider Freshdesk or Zammad. Good feature set including SLA management, automation, and reporting. US-based.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits documented per plan; typically 100-200 req/min

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covers tickets, contacts, staff, and categories. Webhook support for ticket events. No official SDK — standard HTTP Basic auth. Error responses include code and description.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Ticket creation creates new tickets — no deduplication. Update operations are idempotent for same values. Read operations always idempotent. No idempotency key support.
known_agent_gotchas: HTTP Basic auth with key/secret — encode correctly in Authorization header
Custom fields require field IDs from account — not human-readable names
Webhook payloads vary by event type — verify format for each event
Rate limits can trigger during bulk operations — implement backoff

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HTTP Basic auth (key + secret). SOC2 Type II certified. GDPR and HIPAA compliant. US and EU data residency options. Enterprise-grade security for mid-market positioning.
